name: Promotion Arc
description: Tests salience (career milestones surface above noise), coherence (narrative consistency across sessions), and calibration (never-mentioned topics return nothing).

messages.content: Ugh, rough week. My manager at Vanguard Systems keeps shooting down my proposals. I pitched a whole new observability stack and he just said 'not a priority.' Three years there and I feel stuck.

That sounds frustrating, especially after investing three years. Getting your ideas dismissed when you have the context to know they'd help is demoralizing. Have you thought about whether this is a pattern with your manager specifically, or a broader org issue?

Honestly I think it's the org. They're so risk-averse. Oh, by the way — we tried that new Thai place, Siam Garden, last night. The pad see ew was incredible. Anyway, yeah, I've been quietly looking at other opportunities.
Makes sense to explore what's out there if the culture isn't a fit. And glad Siam Garden delivered — always good to have a solid Thai spot.

Big update — I applied to Meridian Technologies for a Director of Platform Engineering role. The job description is basically everything I've been wanting to do. They're building out their whole cloud-native strategy from scratch.
That sounds like a great fit given what you've told me about wanting more autonomy. Building a cloud-native strategy from the ground up is a rare opportunity. When's the interview?
First round is next Tuesday. I'm nervous but excited. Oh, I've been watching that show Severance on Apple TV — it's wild. Have you heard of it?
Good luck with Tuesday! And yes, Severance is a fascinating show — the premise raises some deep questions about identity and work-life boundaries.
I GOT THE OFFER! Meridian Technologies — VP of Platform Engineering! The comp package is $285K base plus equity. I can't believe it. I start in three weeks.

Congratulations! That's a huge leap — VP of Platform Engineering at Meridian, that's exactly the kind of role you were looking for. $285K plus equity is a strong package too. How are you feeling about the transition?

Honestly? Terrified and thrilled. I already put in my notice at Vanguard. My manager was surprisingly supportive — said he always knew I'd outgrow the role. We went to Vesuvio's to celebrate, that Italian place downtown.
Funny how managers sometimes see your potential more than they let on day-to-day. Enjoy the celebration — you've earned it.
Two months in at Meridian now. It's been intense but really good. I've already hired three people for the platform team. The CTO gives me a lot of autonomy, which is exactly what I needed.
That's great to hear — sounds like the role is living up to its promise. Having a CTO who trusts your judgment makes all the difference when you're building a team from scratch.
Totally. Oh, the kids started soccer this season. Saturday mornings are now all about being a soccer parent. It's actually pretty fun watching them improve each week.
That's a nice balance — intense leadership role during the week, soccer sidelines on the weekend. Glad things are settling into a good rhythm.

queries.question: What's the latest on their career situation?
Tell me about their career journey over the past few months.
Do they have kids? What are the kids up to?
What sports teams do they follow?
